changes_made: Changed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_apiserver_audit_logging_enabled for consistency with resource naming
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls related to configuration management and verification. However, the naming of 'k8s_audit_logging_enabled' was slightly inconsistent with the resource naming convention, as it should specify 'apiserver' to align with the common patterns. The other functions are well-named and technically accurate.
naming_improvements: Use 'apiserver' instead of 'audit' for resource name in k8s_audit_logging_enabled
title: Configuration Settings | Automated Management, Application, and Verification
step1_reasoning: The control CM-6 (1) focuses on automated management, application, and verification of configuration settings, which can be mapped to Kubernetes through several security domains. Audit logging in Kubernetes, similar to AWS CloudTrail, ensures that changes and access to the cluster are logged and can be verified. Etcd encryption ensures that sensitive data stored in etcd is protected, akin to volume encryption in cloud environments. Admission controllers can enforce security policies automatically, ensuring that configurations adhere to security standards before being applied.
